primaryRecommendation: Strengthen probability before quantitative projects or interviews.
whyRecommended: The student is targeting quantitative trading, has early programming exposure, and currently lacks probability evidence.
whyBeforeAlternatives: Probability is a prerequisite for simulation projects, research, and interview preparation.
evidenceProduced: Completed probability problem set or simulation notebook.
confidenceExplanation: Moderate confidence because the profile includes major, goal, experience, and time, but limited college evidence.
opportunityRecommendationType: developmental quantitative programs and verified freshman-friendly opportunities

primaryRecommendation: Finish one working, documented programming project.
whyRecommended: The student is early in CS and has no visible project evidence yet.
whyBeforeAlternatives: A finished artifact makes later resume, research, internship, and interview advice more useful.
evidenceProduced: Working code, tests, and a README.
confidenceExplanation: Moderate confidence because the profile clearly identifies CS and missing projects.
opportunityRecommendationType: developmental technical roles and project-based programs

primaryRecommendation: Secure one relevant role with defined responsibilities.
whyRecommended: The student has project evidence but lacks internship or applied role experience.
whyBeforeAlternatives: Experience is the clearest missing signal after projects and resume readiness.
evidenceProduced: Confirmed role or applications to eligible roles.
confidenceExplanation: High confidence because the profile has projects, resume status, stage, and a clear missing experience signal.
opportunityRecommendationType: target and stretch internship, campus job, and research matches

whyRecommended: The student already has internship and project evidence but limited interview preparation.
whyBeforeAlternatives: Junior timing makes interview readiness more urgent than broad exploration.
evidenceProduced: Mock interview feedback log and corrected mistakes.
confidenceExplanation: High confidence because the profile contains stage, prior experience, and interview-prep signal.
opportunityRecommendationType: verified interview-heavy internship and new-grad pipelines

whyRecommended: The student is pre-med with no clinical exposure, shadowing, or volunteering evidence yet.
whyBeforeAlternatives: Clinical exposure tests fit before later application planning and letters.
confidenceExplanation: High confidence because the profile clearly identifies pre-med and missing clinical exposure.
opportunityRecommendationType: healthcare volunteering, shadowing, and campus pre-health resources

whyRecommended: The student has research and clinical exposure but weak sustained service evidence.
whyBeforeAlternatives: Service fills the most visible gap before application review.
evidenceProduced: Ongoing service commitment with defined responsibility.
confidenceExplanation: High confidence because the profile distinguishes research strength from service weakness.
opportunityRecommendationType: verified service, public-health, and clinical-adjacent opportunities

primaryRecommendation: Secure one relevant finance or deal-oriented role with defined responsibilities.
whyRecommended: The student has finance interest and resume readiness but lacks applied finance experience.
whyBeforeAlternatives: Defined finance experience is more useful than broad outreach when recruiting evidence is thin.
evidenceProduced: Applications, interviews, or a confirmed finance role.
confidenceExplanation: Moderate confidence because UnlockED currently maps finance advising to the quantitative/finance framework.
opportunityRecommendationType: finance internships, early insight programs, and campus finance roles

primaryRecommendation: Complete one inquiry-based analytics experience tied to a real question.
whyRecommended: The student has marketing experience but needs stronger analytics evidence.
whyBeforeAlternatives: A focused inquiry artifact is more useful than broad career browsing.
evidenceProduced: Dashboard, analysis memo, or campaign insight summary.
confidenceExplanation: Moderate confidence because the profile is specific but uses the general-exploration framework.
opportunityRecommendationType: analytics projects, campus marketing roles, and research opportunities

primaryRecommendation: Join or create one inquiry-based psychology experience tied to a real question.
whyRecommended: The student is considering clinical psychology and lacks lab or inquiry experience.
whyBeforeAlternatives: Clinical psychology paths require careful fit testing and research exposure rather than medical-school assumptions.
evidenceProduced: Research role, literature review, or analysis sample.
confidenceExplanation: Moderate confidence because clinical psychology support is currently conservative and non-licensing-specific.
opportunityRecommendationType: psychology research, service, and graduate exploration resources

primaryRecommendation: Finish one substantial engineering or technical project before starting another.
whyRecommended: The student has coursework but lacks a visible design or build artifact.
whyBeforeAlternatives: A finished artifact clarifies skills and makes applications easier to evaluate.
evidenceProduced: Portfolio-ready project with photos, files, tests, or design notes.
confidenceExplanation: Moderate confidence because engineering is supported through the general evidence framework.
opportunityRecommendationType: engineering projects, campus labs, design teams, and internships

primaryRecommendation: Run a short career-fit sprint before committing to a narrow path.
whyRecommended: The student is undecided and has several broad interests but no tested direction.
whyBeforeAlternatives: Exploration should come before path-specific projects or applications.
estimatedEffort: 2-4 hours over one to two weeks
evidenceProduced: One-page comparison and next path to test.
confidenceExplanation: Appropriately limited confidence because the profile is intentionally broad.
opportunityRecommendationType: career resources, student organizations, campus jobs, and exploratory programs

primaryRecommendation: Choose a smaller version of the CS evidence-building step that fits two hours weekly.
whyRecommended: The student is interested in software but has fewer than three available hours per week.
whyBeforeAlternatives: The advisor should not recommend a workload that exceeds the student's stated time constraint.
evidenceProduced: Small but complete code artifact, resume draft, or documented learning log.
confidenceExplanation: Moderate confidence because the time constraint is clear and limits recommendation scope.
opportunityRecommendationType: low-time campus resources and saved opportunities for later

primaryRecommendation: Join or create one biology inquiry experience tied to a real research question.
whyRecommended: The student has biology coursework and research interest but no lab or inquiry role yet.
whyBeforeAlternatives: Research evidence is more relevant than clinical exposure when the stated path is graduate or lab research.
evidenceProduced: Lab role, literature review, poster draft, or research summary.
confidenceExplanation: Moderate confidence because the profile clearly names biology research but UnlockED uses a conservative research framework.
opportunityRecommendationType: biology research roles, fellowships, campus labs, and research programs
